Pre-course information to help you make the most of the day.

Jumping Rivers Pre-course material

R with Jumping Rivers

We realise that having to put some ground work in before a course can be frustrating, but to get the most of out our courses, we need you to do a few things before hand:

  1. You must bring a laptop with you.
  2. You must come with the latest version of Rstudio and R available to you via IT. Analysts can download R/RStudio via the Software Centre and if there are issues they should contact CSD.
  3. You must download the course packages, see below for this.

Course Packages

Regardless of which course you are attending, you will need to run these two lines of code in either R or Rstudio

install.packages("drat")
drat::addRepo("jr-packages")

Depending on which course you are attending, you’ll need a different package. Simply scroll to the correct course and run that line of code. If you’re struggling with this either ask CSD or our expert trainers are more than happy to sort issues on the day.

On the day, we do have a cloud fall back, but we’ve found it better for participants if you have the software installed on your laptop.

Introduction to R

install.packages("jrIntroduction")

If all has gone well, then running

library("jrIntroduction")

should give no errors.

Introduction to the Tidyverse

install.packages("jrTidyverse")

If all has gone well, then running

library("jrTidyverse")

should give no errors.

Next Steps in the Tidyverse

install.packages("jrTidyverse2")

If all has gone well, then running

library("jrTidyverse2")

should give no errors.

Advanced Graphics with R

install.packages("jrGgplot2")

If all has gone well, then running

library("jrGgplot2")

should give no errors.
